title: McGill Financial Asset Management Hackathon '24
detail:
QUARCC placed second at the McGill FIAM'24, outperforming many ivy league school's teams, and bringing $10,000 home. 

 By Valentino Magniette-Bosseboeuf, Nicholas Cook, Kenny Chavez, Casey Jussaume, and Lucas Miranda.

Our Ensemble triple transformer and 5 deep learning network architecture for multi-strategy investing!!

In the finale of the McGill-FIAM asset management hackathon, our team(Valentino Magniette-Bosseboeuf Nicholas Cook Kenny Chavez Casey Jussaume Lucas Miranda) built AI analysts for momentum, options, sentiment from SEC filings, fundamentals and whole sector returns, and got 2nd on the podium🏆.


Our analysts were guided by a temporal fusion transformer that acted as Portfolio Manager. This transformer was regime detecting, hardcoded with bull, bear, high volatility and low volatility markets in mind.

It was also meta-learning, it could trust certain AI analysts more in specific regimes and create its own regimes and improve the fund’s strategies over time.

The fundamentals and momentum analysts found patterns in company stock data and used their strategies to beat the market.

The options analyst used sentiment in the option chain to beat our benchmark and confirm Russ Goyenko’s research papers on the subject - options data indeed outperforms stock data.

Due to the fact that we did not have labelled data across sectors, we have to procedurally cluster the stocks in 10 sectors with a custom kmeans implementation, to not group two different sectors together. It trailed our benchmark with exceedingly low risk.

Our smartest AI analyst was technically 2. In order to save on computation costs we built a model to summarize textual data from SEC 10-k annual reports, quarterly reports and form-4s to then feed them to our tuned BERT model to predict the next months returns. « Company ABC’s revenues grew by 22% and growth is slated to continue on that pace, multiple executives are buying in. »
It beat the market surprisingly more than we had anticipated. 

In short, we brought a tank to a knife fight, and due to time constraints it had trouble fitting through the door! Our team used the least libraries as we could and optimized for stability and got the BEST risk-adjusted returns. 📈 

Thank you to everyone involved for providing this learning opportunity. Especially our mentor Louis-Philippe🫡
